```{r, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE, fig.width=9.5, fig.height=4.8, out.width='100%'}
  "family_baseline_political_participation_trust"
)

endline_participation_model <- run_endline_family_model(
  endline_plot_base,
  "family_political_participation",
  "family_baseline_political_participation_trust"
)

endline_stacked_direct_model <- feols(
  y ~ mayoral_discretion_index +
    baseline + log(pop07nso) + pi + land + bgytot + vote_diff + dynasty_next_mayor |
    pairnum + family |
    share_cycles_complete + share_cycles_complete:mayoral_discretion_index ~
    treat + treat:mayoral_discretion_index,
  cluster = ~mun_psgc,
  data = welfare_family_dat |>
    filter(sample == "Endline", family %in% c("Service access", "Economic welfare"))
)

endline_main_plot_df <- bind_rows(
  family_results |>
    filter(
      family_label %in% c(
        "Endline: Official assistance",
        "Endline: Economic welfare",
        "Endline: Service access"
      )
    ),
  extract_endline_term(endline_civic_model, "Endline: Civic efficacy"),
  extract_endline_term(endline_participation_model, "Endline: Political participation"),
  extract_endline_term(endline_stacked_direct_model, "Endline: Pooled direct welfare")
) |>
  mutate(
    family_label = factor(
      family_label,
      levels = c(
        "Endline: Pooled direct welfare",
        "Endline: Service access",
        "Endline: Economic welfare",
        "Endline: Official assistance",
        "Endline: Civic efficacy",
        "Endline: Political participation"
      ),
      labels = c(
        "Pooled direct welfare",
        "Service access",
        "Economic welfare",
        "Official assistance",
        "Civic efficacy",
        "Political participation"
      )
    ),
    p_label = sprintf("p = %.3f", p)
  )

xmin_end <- min(endline_main_plot_df$ci_low, na.rm = TRUE)
xmax_end <- max(endline_main_plot_df$ci_high, na.rm = TRUE)
xrange_end <- xmax_end - xmin_end

endline_main_plot_df <- endline_main_plot_df |>
  mutate(p_x = xmin_end - 0.22 * xrange_end)

ggplot(endline_main_plot_df, aes(x = estimate, y = family_label)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = ci_low, xmax = ci_high), height = 0.18, color = "gray35") +
  geom_point(color = "#1F5A7A", size = 2.8) +
  geom_text(aes(x = p_x, label = p_label), hjust = 0, size = 3.2, color = "gray20") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(xmin_end - 0.30 * xrange_end, xmax_end + 0.05 * xrange_end)) +
  labs(
    x = "Coefficient on treatment intensity x mayoral discretion",
    y = NULL,
    title = "Figure 4: Endline welfare gains contract where mayors have greater discretion",
    subtitle = "IV estimates with matched-pair fixed effects, municipal covariates, and 95% confidence intervals"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(hjust = 1)
  )
```

\clearpage

# 7 Conclusion

The relationship between external assistance and state capacity remains a central puzzle in political economy. Classical theories of aid fungibility predict that grants displace local spending, yet empirical evidence is inconsistent. In this paper, I ask not only whether aid substitutes for public spending but what politicians do with the resources it frees, and under what institutional conditions these dynamics obtain. I advance the debate on three fronts: showing that substitution extends beyond intergovernmental transfers to non-governmental programs that bypass public accounts entirely; demonstrating that democratic competition determines whether crowd-out occurs at all; and tracing the political destination of freed resources through procurement and labor market data.

Leveraging a randomized evaluation of the World Bank–financed Kalahi-CIDSS program in the Philippines, I provide causal evidence that non-governmental development assistance crowds out local public spending, that this effect is concentrated in low-competition municipalities where incumbents face limited electoral accountability, and that freed resources are not simply consumed as fiscal slack. In uncompetitive settings, treated mayors directed procurement contracts toward electorally supportive villages and expanded casual government employment, two canonical instruments of clientelism. In competitive municipalities, by contrast, mayors maintained spending levels and allocated contracts programmatically across villages. Democratic accountability shaped not only the level of public spending but the distributional logic of how freed resources were deployed.

The welfare analysis offers suggestive evidence that these fiscal dynamics bore consequences for human development. Re-examining survey data from the impact evaluation with an index of fiscal discretion capturing the extent to which mayors could feasibly substitute Kalahi for municipal effort, I find that welfare effects attenuate with discretion. Furthermore, mayoral discretion undermined Kalahi's capacity to promote social trust and trust in participatory governance. The program worked best, in other words, when politicians were least able to undermine it.

These results carry clear implications for development policy. Bypassing government channels may insulate aid from direct capture, but it does not eliminate the political incentives of incumbents to exploit the fiscal space that external provision creates. Aid programs operating in low-accountability environments should anticipate substitution effects and design monitoring mechanisms that hold local governments accountable for maintaining co-investment in program-relevant domains. More broadly, the governance context of aid recipients deserves as much analytical attention as the technical design of the programs themselves. Where democratic institutions are weak, the effectiveness of development assistance depends not only on program quality but on whether the political environment permits its gains to survive.

\clearpage

# References

::: {#refs}
:::

\clearpage

# Appendix

\setcounter{table}{0}
\renewcommand{\thetable}{A\arabic{table}}
\setcounter{figure}{0}
\renewcommand{\thefigure}{A\arabic{figure}}

```{r, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
# Pre-treatment balance via randomization inference

suppressMessages({
  library(DeclareDesign)
  library(lmtest)
  library(ri2)
  library(nnet)
})

set.seed(214)

balance_vars <- c("kalahi_spending", 
                  "total_spending", 
                  "kalahi_spending_percap", 
                  "total_spending_percap",
                  "pi", 
                  "bgytot", 
                  "pop07nso", 
                  "land")

balance_df <- historic_spend %>%
  filter(year < 2011) |> 
  select(pairnum, province, treat, all_of(balance_vars)) %>% 
  na.omit

declaration <- 
  with(balance_df,{
    declare_ra(
      blocks = province,
      clusters = pairnum,
      conditions = 0:1
    )
  })

# Robust regression function
regression_fun <- function(data) {
   lm_robust(as.formula(paste0(
     "treat ~ as.factor(pairnum) + ",
     paste0(balance_vars, collapse = "+"))),
     clusters = province, data = data
     )
  }

balance_fun <- function(data) {
  data %>% regression_fun %>% glance %>% pull(statistic)
  }

ri2_out <-
  conduct_ri(
    test_function = balance_fun,
    declaration = declaration,
    assignment = "treat",
    sharp_hypothesis = 0,
    sims = 100,
    data = balance_df
    )

ri2_summ <- summary(ri2_out) %>% rename_with(~paste0(., "_F_ri"))

balance_results <- balance_df %>% 
  regression_fun() %>% 
  tidy %>% 
  filter(term %in% balance_vars) %>% 
  bind_cols(ri2_summ)

# Present results
historic_treat <- historic_spend |> filter(treat == 1 & year < 2011)
historic_cont <- historic_spend |> filter(treat == 0 & year < 2011)

data.frame(
  var = c("public service spending", "total spending", "poverty index", "num barangay", "population",
          "land area"),
  mean_treat = c(mean(historic_treat$kalahi_spending / historic_treat$pop07nso),
                 mean(historic_treat$total_spending / historic_treat$pop07nso),
                 mean(historic_treat$pi), mean(historic_treat$bgytot),
                 mean(historic_treat$pop07nso), mean(historic_treat$land)),
  mean_cont = c(mean(historic_cont$kalahi_spending / historic_cont$pop07nso),
                mean(historic_cont$total_spending / historic_cont$pop07nso),
                mean(historic_cont$pi), mean(historic_cont$bgytot),
                mean(historic_cont$pop07nso), mean(historic_cont$land)),
  sd_treat = c(sd(historic_treat$kalahi_spending / historic_treat$pop07nso),
               sd(historic_treat$total_spending / historic_treat$pop07nso),
               sd(historic_treat$pi), sd(historic_treat$bgytot),
               sd(historic_treat$pop07nso), sd(historic_treat$land)),
  sd_cont = c(sd(historic_cont$kalahi_spending / historic_cont$pop07nso),
              sd(historic_cont$total_spending / historic_cont$pop07nso),
              sd(historic_cont$pi), sd(historic_cont$bgytot),
              sd(historic_cont$pop07nso), sd(historic_cont$land)),
  p = c(balance_results[1,5], balance_results[2,5], balance_results[3,5],
        balance_results[4,5], balance_results[5,5], balance_results[6,5])
) |>
  kable(
    format = "latex",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    digits = 4,
    caption = "Pretreatment covariate balance"
  ) |>
  kable_styling(font_size = 8, position = "center", latex_options = "HOLD_position")
```

\clearpage

```{r echo=FALSE, fig.height=6.4, fig.width=9, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, out.width='100%'}
# Appendix Figure A1: pre-treatment balance on spending outcomes

appendix_balance_plot_dat <- historic_spending |>
  filter(year >= 2007, year < 2011) |>
  transmute(
    year,
    treat,
    public_service_spending = kalahi_spending_percap,
    total_spending = total_spending_percap
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(public_service_spending, total_spending),
    names_to = "outcome",
    values_to = "value"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    outcome = recode(
      outcome,
      public_service_spending = "Public service spending",
      total_spending = "Total spending"
    )
  ) |>
  group_by(year, outcome) |>
  summarise(
    estimate = mean(value[treat == 1], na.rm = TRUE) - mean(value[treat == 0], na.rm = TRUE),
    se = sqrt(
      stats::var(value[treat == 1], na.rm = TRUE) / sum(treat == 1 & !is.na(value)) +
      stats::var(value[treat == 0], na.rm = TRUE) / sum(treat == 0 & !is.na(value))
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    ci_low = estimate - 1.96 * se,
    ci_high = estimate + 1.96 * se
  )

ggplot(appendix_balance_plot_dat, aes(x = year, y = estimate)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray55", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_linerange(aes(ymin = ci_low, ymax = ci_high), color = "#1F4E79", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_line(color = "#1F4E79", linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_point(color = "#1F4E79", size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~ outcome, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 2007:2010) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Treated minus control mean (PhP per capita)",
    title = "Appendix Figure A1: Pre-treatment balance on municipal spending outcomes",
    subtitle = "Annual treated-control differences with 95% confidence intervals, pre-treatment years only"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 9.5),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```

\clearpage

```{r, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
# Appendix Table A2: Main crowd-out robustness to outcome construction
spend_robust <- spend |>
  mutate(
    asinh_kalahi_spending_pc = asinh(kalahi_spending_percap),
    kalahi_spending_share = ifelse(total_spending_percap > 0, kalahi_spending_percap / total_spending_percap, NA_real_),
    z_kalahi_spending_pc = as.numeric(scale(kalahi_spending_percap))
  )

outcome_main <- feols(
  kalahi_spending_percap ~ treat + yolanda + border_treat + log(pop07nso) + pi + land + bgytot |
    province + quarter,
  data = spend_robust,
  cluster = ~ province^quarter
)

outcome_asinh <- feols(
  asinh_kalahi_spending_pc ~ treat + yolanda + border_treat + log(pop07nso) + pi + land + bgytot |
    province + quarter,
  data = spend_robust,
  cluster = ~ province^quarter
)

outcome_share <- feols(
  kalahi_spending_share ~ treat + yolanda + border_treat + log(pop07nso) + pi + land + bgytot |
    province + quarter,
  data = spend_robust,
  cluster = ~ province^quarter
)

outcome_z <- feols(
  z_kalahi_spending_pc ~ treat + yolanda + border_treat + log(pop07nso) + pi + land + bgytot |
    province + quarter,
  data = spend_robust,
  cluster = ~ province^quarter
)

modelsummary(
  list(
    "Per-capita level" = outcome_main,
    "asinh(per-capita)" = outcome_asinh,
    "Per-capita share of total" = outcome_share,
    "z-score per-capita" = outcome_z
  ),
  coef_map = c("treat" = "Kalahi treatment"),
  stars = TRUE,
  title = "Appendix Table A2: Crowd-out robustness to outcome construction",
  output = "kableExtra"
) |>
  kable_classic(latex_options = "scale_down") |>
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, position = "center") |>
  footnote(
    general = "Outcome construction varies by column. All models include province and quarter fixed effects with province-by-quarter clustered standard errors.",
    threeparttable = TRUE,
    escape = FALSE,
    footnote_as_chunk = TRUE
  )
```

\clearpage

```{r}
# Appendix Table A3: Crowd-out effect robust to restricting  sample restricted to pre-KC-NCCDP period

#Restrict sample
spend_preKCNCCDP <- spend |> filter(year < 2015)

# Estimate models
base_kalahi_preKCNCCDP <- feols(
  kalahi_spending_percap ~ treat +
    yolanda + dynasty_next_mayor + log(pop07nso) + pi + land + bgytot |
    pairnum + quarter,
  data = spend_preKCNCCDP,
  cluster = ~ pairnum^quarter
)

base_total_preKCNCCDP <- feols(
  total_spending_percap ~ treat +
    yolanda + dynasty_next_mayor + log(pop07nso) + pi + land + bgytot |
    pairnum + quarter,
  data = spend_preKCNCCDP,
  cluster = ~ pairnum^quarter
)

moderator_preKCNCCDP <- feols(
  kalahi_spending_percap ~ treat * competition_z +
    yolanda + log(pop07nso) + pi + land + bgytot |
    pairnum + quarter,
  data = spend_preKCNCCDP,
  cluster = ~ pairnum^quarter
)

casual_preKCNCCDP <- feols(
  gov_short_term ~ treat +
    as.factor(female) + as.numeric(age) + pi + log(pop07nso) + land + bgytot |
    pairnum,
  cluster = ~ pairnum,
  data = lfs |> filter(year < 2015)
)

modelsummary(
  list(
    "Kalahi spending" = base_kalahi_preKCNCCDP,
    "Total spending" = base_total_preKCNCCDP,
    "Spending by competition" = moderator_preKCNCCDP,
    "Casual employment" = casual_preKCNCCDP
  ),
  coef_map = c(
    "treat" = "treat",
    "treat:competiton_z" = "treat x competition"
  ),
  stars = TRUE,
  title = "Non-governmental development programs crowd-out public spending",
  output = "kableExtra"
) |>
  kable_classic(latex_options = "scale_down") |>
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, position = "center") |>
  footnote(
    general = "Spending outcomes measured in Philippine pesos (PhP) per capita.",
    threeparttable = TRUE,
    escape = FALSE,
    footnote_as_chunk = TRUE
  )
```

```{r, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
# Appendix Table A4: Moderator robustness for competition construction
spend_mod_robust <- spend |>
  mutate(
    competition_raw = -vote_diff,
    competition_rank = percent_rank(competition_raw),
    close_10 = ifelse(vote_diff <= 0.10, 1, 0),
    close_20 = ifelse(vote_diff <= 0.20, 1, 0)
  )

mod_z <- feols(
  kalahi_spending_percap ~ treat * competition_z +
    yolanda + border_treat + log(pop07nso) + pi + land + bgytot |
    province + quarter,
  data = spend_mod_robust,
  cluster = ~ province^quarter
)

mod_c <- feols(
  kalahi_spending_percap ~ treat * competition_c +
    yolanda + border_treat + log(pop07nso) + pi + land + bgytot |
    province + quarter,
  data = spend_mod_robust,
  cluster = ~ province^quarter
)

mod_rank <- feols(
  kalahi_spending_percap ~ treat * competition_rank +
    yolanda + border_treat + log(pop07nso) + pi + land + bgytot |
    province + quarter,
  data = spend_mod_robust,
  cluster = ~ province^quarter
)

mod_close10 <- feols(
  kalahi_spending_percap ~ treat * close_10 +
    yolanda + border_treat + log(pop07nso) + pi + land + bgytot |
    province + quarter,
  data = spend_mod_robust,
  cluster = ~ province^quarter
)

mod_close20 <- feols(
  kalahi_spending_percap ~ treat * close_20 +
    yolanda + border_treat + log(pop07nso) + pi + land + bgytot |
    province + quarter,
  data = spend_mod_robust,
  cluster = ~ province^quarter
)

modelsummary(
  list(
    "z-scored competition" = mod_z,
    "centered competition" = mod_c,
    "rank competition" = mod_rank,
    "close election (10pp)" = mod_close10,
    "close election (20pp)" = mod_close20
  ),
  coef_map = c(
    "treat" = "Kalahi treatment",
    "treat:competition_z" = "Treat x competition (z)",
    "treat:competition_c" = "Treat x competition (centered)",
    "treat:competition_rank" = "Treat x competition (rank)",
    "treat:close_10" = "Treat x close election (10pp)",
    "treat:close_20" = "Treat x close election (20pp)"
  ),
  stars = TRUE,
  title = "Appendix Table A4: Competition moderator robustness",
  output = "kableExtra"
) |>
  kable_classic(latex_options = "scale_down") |>
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, position = "center") |>
  footnote(
    general = "Each column uses an alternative competition construction in the interaction with treatment. All models include province and quarter fixed effects with province-by-quarter clustered standard errors.",
    threeparttable = TRUE,
    escape = FALSE,
    footnote_as_chunk = TRUE
  )
```

\clearpage

```{r, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE, fig.width=9, fig.height=4.8, out.width='100%'}
# Appendix Figure A2: stacked direct-welfare moderation within wave

appendix_direct_welfare_dat <- welfare_family_dat |>
  filter(family %in% c("Service access", "Economic welfare"))

appendix_stacked_midline_model <- feols(
  y ~ treat * mayoral_discretion_index +
    baseline + log(pop07nso) + pi + land + bgytot + vote_diff + dynasty_next_mayor |
    pairnum + family,
  cluster = ~mun_psgc,
  data = appendix_direct_welfare_dat |>
    filter(sample == "Midline")
)

appendix_stacked_endline_model <- feols(
  y ~ mayoral_discretion_index +
    baseline + log(pop07nso) + pi + land + bgytot + vote_diff + dynasty_next_mayor |
    pairnum + family |
    share_cycles_complete + share_cycles_complete:mayoral_discretion_index ~
    treat + treat:mayoral_discretion_index,
  cluster = ~mun_psgc,
  data = appendix_direct_welfare_dat |>
    filter(sample == "Endline")
)

appendix_stacked_results <- bind_rows(
  tibble(
    wave = "Midline",
    estimate = coeftable(appendix_stacked_midline_model)["treat:mayoral_discretion_index", "Estimate"],
    se = coeftable(appendix_stacked_midline_model)["treat:mayoral_discretion_index", "Std. Error"],
    p = coeftable(appendix_stacked_midline_model)["treat:mayoral_discretion_index", "Pr(>|t|)"]
  ),
  tibble(
    wave = "Endline",
    estimate = coeftable(appendix_stacked_endline_model)["fit_share_cycles_complete:mayoral_discretion_index", "Estimate"],
    se = coeftable(appendix_stacked_endline_model)["fit_share_cycles_complete:mayoral_discretion_index", "Std. Error"],
    p = coeftable(appendix_stacked_endline_model)["fit_share_cycles_complete:mayoral_discretion_index", "Pr(>|t|)"]
  )
) |>
  mutate(
    ci_low = estimate - 1.96 * se,
    ci_high = estimate + 1.96 * se,
    wave = factor(wave, levels = c("Endline", "Midline")),
    p_label = sprintf("p = %.3f", p)
  )

xmin_stack <- min(appendix_stacked_results$ci_low, na.rm = TRUE)
xmax_stack <- max(appendix_stacked_results$ci_high, na.rm = TRUE)
xrange_stack <- xmax_stack - xmin_stack

appendix_stacked_results <- appendix_stacked_results |>
  mutate(p_x = xmin_stack - 0.22 * xrange_stack)

ggplot(appendix_stacked_results, aes(x = estimate, y = wave)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = ci_low, xmax = ci_high), height = 0.18, color = "gray35") +
  geom_point(aes(color = wave), size = 2.8) +
  geom_text(aes(x = p_x, label = p_label), hjust = 0, size = 3.2, color = "gray20") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Midline" = "#8C2F39", "Endline" = "#1F5A7A"), guide = "none") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(xmin_stack - 0.30 * xrange_stack, xmax_stack + 0.05 * xrange_stack)) +
  labs(
    x = "Average moderation across Service access and Economic welfare",
    y = NULL,
    title = "Figure A2: Stacked direct-welfare moderation within wave",
    subtitle = "Midline OLS and endline IV estimates with family fixed effects; 95% confidence intervals"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(hjust = 1)
  )
```

\clearpage

\begin{landscape}

```{r, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
# Appendix Table A5: Welfare-family component mapping

family_map <- tibble::tribble(
  ~Wave, ~Family, ~Component_Outcomes,
  "Midline", "Service access", "Distance to public services; travel time to public services; travel cost to public services",
  "Endline", "Service access", "Distance to public services; travel time to public services; amount paid for water; time spent fetching water; number of water trips",
  "Midline", "Economic welfare", "No Yolanda hardship; no other hardship",
  "Endline", "Economic welfare", "Household spending; labor income",
  "Midline", "Official assistance", "Official source of Yolanda support; official source of other hardship support; support received for Yolanda hardship; support received for other hardship",
  "Endline", "Official assistance", "Asked LGU official for help; asked congress representative for help; received cash assistance; received in-kind assistance",
  "Midline", "Political participation and trust", "Trust in people inside barangay; trust in people outside barangay; mayor performance; mayor perceived cleanliness; mayor honesty",
  "Endline", "Political participation and trust", "Collective confidence; comfort speaking in assemblies; likelihood of talking to barangay captain; perceived ability to improve barangay; attended municipal council meeting; contacted politician; filed complaint; assembly not dominated by elites",
  "Midline", "Informal support", "Family/household would help if sick; neighbors would help; self-help; well-off community members would help; volunteers would help; relatives would help; anyone in barangay would help; friends would help; priests would help; no one would help (reverse coded)",
  "Endline", "Informal support", "Asked family for help; asked friends for help; asked community leader for help; did not need outside help (reverse of no outside help)"
)

kable(
  family_map,
  format = "latex",
  booktabs = TRUE,
  longtable = TRUE,
  linesep = "",
  escape = TRUE,
  col.names = c("Wave", "Family", "Survey outcomes")
) |>
  kable_styling(
    position = "center",
    font_size = 8,
    latex_options = c("repeat_header")
  ) |>
  column_spec(1, width = "1.1in") |>
  column_spec(2, width = "1.8in") |>
  column_spec(3, width = "6.8in") |>
  footnote(
    general = "Entries describe the component outcomes used to construct each wave-specific welfare-family index. All components are oriented so that higher values indicate better welfare, broader access, or stronger support before standardization and averaging.",
    threeparttable = FALSE,
    escape = FALSE,
    footnote_as_chunk = TRUE
  )
```

\end{landscape}
